---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-worldmap3.png')
background-size: 105%
background-class: top
class: middle

.size45[**I. Arguments, Evidence and International Relations**]

<br>

.size45[

**Today's Agenda**

Review outlines and write the paper.
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2024)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Check submissions on Canvas
    - Review and give feedback during class




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.pull-left[

<br>
<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**For Today**]]

<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**Draft an Outline**]]

]

.pull-right[

.size25[**Intro**
+ What is the question?
+ Why do we care?
+ Thesis statement
]

.size25[**Reason 1 (premise)**
+ Evidence x 2
]

.size25[**Reason 2 (premise)**
+ Evidence x 2
]

.size25[**Reason 3 (premise)**
+ Evidence x 2
]]


???

### Everybody ready to go with this?

#### - Outline in hand?

<br>

Ok, time to get some feedback!

- Today you will get feedback from two people and have a chance to revise your outline to reflect that feedback.

- In a moment I'll split you into pairs...

<br>

**SLIDE**: Plan of attack!




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size55[.center[**Review the Outlines in Pairs**]]

### Person 1: Present your Outline
.size35[
+ What is the question? 
+ Why do we care?
+ Thesis
+ Each premise with evidence
]

???

Person 1: Present the outline of your paper

- Each item should be a complete sentence
    - or two for the why do we care question

- Each premise should be stated and then supported by evidence

--

### Person 2: To be convincing to you...
.size35[
+ Is each item clear?
+ Is each premise logical?
+ Is each piece of evidence high enough quality?
]

???

<br>

Person 2 gives feedback focused on helping person 1 make it more convincing

- Is each item clear?
    - e.g. Does the sentence convey the idea in a simple and accessible way?

- Is each premise logical?
    - e.g. does it directly support the conclusion?

- Is each piece of evidence high enough quality?

<br>

### Make sense?

<br>

**SLIDE**: I also want you to do one more thing!



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size55[.center[**Review the Outlines in Pairs**]]

### Person 1: Present your Outline
.size35[
+ What is the question? 
+ Why do we care?
+ Thesis
+ Each premise with evidence
]

### Person 2: To be convincing to you...
.size35[
+ Is each item clear?
+ Is each premise logical?
+ Is each piece of evidence high enough quality?
+ .textblue[**Identify a counter-argument for each premise**]
]

???

Let's talk about that counter-argument piece (which is new!)

- Per the rubric, a convincing argument paper MUST include critical analysis!

- This is typically some acknowledgement of the limits of your own argument

1. Include and address a counter-argument
    - Author X (2020) argues that... However, ...
    - To be convincing it MUST always be the STRONGEST version of their argument
    - No straw men! No "Some people argue"...
    
2. Acknowledging limitations on your evidence
    - All measurement is done with error
    - You can boost the depth of your argument by acknowledging how your evidence might be lacking, but making clear why that doesn't mess up your argument
    
<br>

For today, I want you to help each other brainstorm a counter-argument for each premise

- e.g. Here's why my argument might be wrong! And here's how I've addressed those concerns!

- In the actual paper, you'll need to be able to cite evidence for the counter-arguments, but for today we can just generate them

<br>

### Make sense?

<br>

*FOR YOU, SAVE TIME TO DO THIS EXERCISE 2x*



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.pull-left[

<br>

<br>

<br>

.size70[.center[**Revise your Outline**]]

]

.pull-right[

.size35[
**Intro**

**Premise 1** (with evidence)

**Premise 2** (with evidence)

**Premise 3** (with evidence)

**Counter-Argument**

**Conclusion**
]]

???

Now that you've heard some more arguments and gotten feedback on yours it's time to revise.

<br>

Reflect on the feedback, revise your outline and start writing!

- Tighten up those premises

- Add a counter-argument paragraph

<br>

Paper is due at the end of the week!
